title: Vandenboschia mettenii
taxonomy: Vandenboschia mettenii is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia, including Indonesia and Malaysia. The classification of Vandenboschia mettenii includes its placement within the kingdom Plantae, division Pteridophyta, and class Polypodiopsida. This fern species is often found growing in shaded, humid environments with high levels of rainfall. Its classification as a member of the family Dryopteridaceae places it among other similar fern species found in tropical regions.
uses: Vandenboschia mettenii, also known as the New Zealand fan fern, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant is said to have antiseptic properties and can be used to treat wounds and skin infections.
